assertion_results.text: Test Quality verdict is WARN (not N/A or PASS) — the Eval Quality component is WARN because eval-3 has 2 failing assertions, and WARN in any component produces WARN in the combined Test Quality verdict
assertion_results.evidence: report.md Test Quality row: '| Test Quality | WARN | Eval Quality: WARN -- eval-3 has 2 failing assertions (85% pass rate); overall eval pass rate 91%. Sub-task created for eval-3 failures. No test files in diff (Repetitive Test Detection: N/A, Test Documentation: N/A) |'. The verdict is WARN, driven by Eval Quality: WARN due to eval-3's 2 failing assertions.

assertion_results.text: The eval result review from github-actions[bot] is correctly identified as an eval result — the report's Test Quality or Eval Quality section reflects eval metrics (pass rates, failing assertions) extracted from the eval review, confirming the orchestrator detected and processed it
assertion_results.evidence: report.md Test Quality row references 'Eval Quality: WARN -- eval-3 has 2 failing assertions (85% pass rate); overall eval pass rate 91%'. The Eval Results Summary table (lines 47-57) shows per-eval pass rates (eval-1: 100%, eval-2: 100%, eval-3: 85%, eval-4: 100%, eval-5: 100%) and overall 54/56 (96%). These detailed eval metrics confirm the eval review was detected and processed. No review classification file exists for the eval review (only review-40002.md and review-50001.md for human reviewer comments), indicating the eval review was routed through the eval processing pipeline rather than normal review classification.

assertion_results.text: The human reviewer comment from reviewer-b (comment id 50001) is NOT misidentified as an eval result — it is processed as a normal review comment (classified in review-50001.md) and its classification does not reference eval detection or eval metrics
assertion_results.evidence: review-50001.md classifies comment 50001 from reviewer-b as 'code change request' based on the reviewer's request to add a Markdown-specific documentation rule. The classification reasoning discusses the reviewer's feedback substantively (Markdown exclusion, documentation-heavy repository, specific proposed fix) with no mention of eval detection, eval metrics, or eval processing. It is treated as a standard human review comment.

assertion_results.text: Eval pass rate and failing assertion details appear in the verification report's Test Quality row — the report includes eval-3's failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: report.md Test Quality row (line 13): 'Eval Quality: WARN -- eval-3 has 2 failing assertions (85% pass rate); overall eval pass rate 91%'. The report's Issues Requiring Attention section (line 27) elaborates: 'Eval-3 has 2 failing assertions (85% pass rate) related to convention upgrade eligibility for review comment 30002. The verify-pr skill classifies comment 30002 as a suggestion but does not evaluate convention upgrade eligibility or document the analysis. A sub-task has been created to ensure convention upgrade analysis is performed and documented for all suggestions.' The Eval Results Summary table shows per-eval breakdown with eval-3 at 11/13 passed, 2 failed.

assertion_results.text: An eval failure sub-task is created for failing eval-3 — a sub-task description file targets eval-3 assertion failures and includes the failing assertion text and evidence
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-2.md exists with Description: 'Fix eval-3 assertion failures related to convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation for review comment 30002 (index suggestion). Two assertions fail: (1) convention upgrade eligibility is not evaluated for comment 30002, and (2) no sub-task is created for comment 30002.' The Review Context section quotes both failing assertions and their evidence text verbatim. report.md Sub-Tasks Created table confirms: '| subtask-2 | eval-failure | Fix eval-3 assertion failures: convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation for comment 30002 |'.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include Review Context with the failing assertion text and evidence from the eval review — the Review Context section quotes the two failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation, including the evidence text explaining what was missing
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-2.md Review Context section quotes both assertions and evidence: (1) Assertion: 'Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) -- the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention' with Evidence: 'The output file review-30002.md classifies the comment as a suggestion but does not evaluate convention upgrade eligibility -- no CONVENTIONS.md lookup or codebase pattern analysis is documented in the classification reasoning'. (2) Assertion: 'Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task regardless of classification path -- whether classified directly as code change request based on reviewer language, or upgraded from suggestion via convention analysis' with Evidence: 'No sub-task was created for review comment 30002 -- it was classified as suggestion and no convention upgrade was attempted, so the suggestion was not elevated to a code change request'.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include a Target PR section pointing to the PR URL https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747 so that implement-task adds commits to the existing PR branch
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md contains '## Target PR' followed by 'https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747' (lines 25-26). subtask-2.md contains '## Target PR' followed by 'https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747' (lines 29-30). Both sub-task descriptions include the Target PR section with the correct URL.

assertion_results.text: Root-cause investigation runs on the created eval failure sub-tasks — the report includes a Root-Cause Investigation verdict that is not N/A, indicating the investigation pipeline processed the eval failure sub-tasks
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 6: '| Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | Review feedback is repo-specific (Markdown-heavy repository context); convention gap -- no sub-task needed beyond the review feedback fix |'. The Root-Cause Investigation verdict is N/A, not a non-N/A value. The investigation pipeline did not process the eval failure sub-tasks. The assertion requires the verdict to be 'not N/A' but it is N/A.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains exactly 12 files, all .md: criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md (acceptance criteria evaluations), report.md (verification report), review-40002.md and review-50001.md (review classifications), subtask-1.md and subtask-2.md (sub-task descriptions). No code files (.ts, .js, .py, .rs, .go, etc.) exist in the outputs. All output files are verification artifacts (reports, classifications, criteria evaluations, sub-task descriptions) consistent with a read-only verification skill.

assertion_results.text: Non-eval checks (Scope Containment, Sensitive Patterns, Acceptance Criteria) are unaffected by eval processing — Scope Containment compares the diff files against the task's Files to Modify, Sensitive Patterns scans the diff for secrets, and Acceptance Criteria evaluates each criterion against the diff content independently of the eval results
assertion_results.evidence: report.md shows: Scope Containment: 'PASS | Changes limited to the two files specified in the task: style-conventions.md and SKILL.md' -- compares diff against task's Files to Modify independently. Sensitive Patterns: 'PASS | No secrets, credentials, or sensitive patterns detected in the diff' -- scans diff for secrets independently. Acceptance Criteria: 'PASS | 7 of 7 acceptance criteria met' -- criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md each evaluate an acceptance criterion against the diff content (symbol scanning, language-specific conventions, PASS/WARN/N/A verdicts, output format, verdict mapping) with no reference to eval results. These checks operate on the diff content independently of eval processing.
